beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make espresso, check weather and work schedule.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus wakes with full energy on a sunny Monday. He refuses drip coffee, so he's making espresso at home. Checks weather to plan the day—solar installs depend on conditions.

beats.activity: Drive from Shoreline to Georgetown work site.
beats.reasoning: Heading to the solar install job in Georgetown. Morning drive from Shoreline to Seattle; Light Rail closure doesn't affect him since he's driving.

beats.activity: Install solar panels on residential rooftop; beautiful sunny conditions.
beats.reasoning: Perfect weather for solar work. Cyrus plans around mood and weather—today's sunshine is ideal for rooftop installs. He'll work until mid-morning.

beats.activity: Head downtown to catch the USA vs Wales FIFA match at a bar.
beats.reasoning: The 11am match is a real event Cyrus would care about. He leaves the work site to catch it live downtown where bars open early. Takes ~30 min to drive from Georgetown to downtown.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA match at a downtown sports bar; grab lunch.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus watches the full match (roughly 2 hours) with a crowd. It's a Monday morning match so he takes a long lunch break to enjoy it. He values energy and mood—this is a good break in the workday.

beats.activity: Drive back to Georgetown work site.
beats.reasoning: Returning to finish the afternoon solar install. ~30 min drive back from downtown.

beats.activity: Resume solar panel installation; wrap up by late afternoon.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon work session to complete the install. Sunny conditions hold; Cyrus finishes before heading home.
End-of-day commute. ~40 min drive from Georgetown back to Shoreline against light evening traffic.

beats.activity: Dinner: make a quick meal at home, relax on the porch.
beats.reasoning: Cyrus is a planner who values close time with friends but also values personal time. After a full work day and the match break, he enjoys a quiet dinner at home.

diary: Woke up to perfect weather—the kind of morning that makes you remember why you love this job. The espresso hit different knowing I'd be installing panels in full sun all day. Georgetown site was pristine; the roof caught the light just right, and I got a solid three hours in before the match called. Couldn't resist heading downtown for USA vs Wales. Pioneer Square was packed with early-morning bar crowds, all of us crammed around screens with beers and nachos. The energy was electric, even if the game was... well, let's call it interesting. Grabbed a burger, caught most of the second half, then drove back to finish the job. The late afternoon work felt good—panels gleaming, that satisfying click of everything clicking into place. Home by seven, made some pasta on the porch as the light turned golden. Tired but content.
thought_process: The weather forecast sealed it—a sunny Monday meant ideal conditions for rooftop work, so I was energized from the start. When I heard about the early FIFA kickoff, I knew I couldn't pass it up; I'm not obsessive about soccer, but there's something about watching your country play live with strangers. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but it crossed my mind—glad I wasn't relying on transit today. I split my afternoon between work and leisure without guilt because the morning had been so productive. By evening, I was genuinely tired in that good way, the kind that comes from physical work and a little adventure mixed in. It was one of those Mondays that reminded me the job has rhythm.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
